title: Infosys Extends Strategic Collaboration with University of Nottingham for Student Management System Digital Infrastructure
detailed_summary: Infosys and the University of Nottingham announced an extension of their strategic collaboration to support high performance and security compliance for the university's critical Student Management System. The relationship builds on collaboration since 2017, under which Infosys supported a digital transformation of the Student Information System that reduced incident volumes by 70 percent, improved system availability and performance by approximately 30 percent, reduced infrastructure costs by over 35 percent, and improved user experience. Under the extended collaboration, Infosys will maintain NottinghamHub, enhance student lifecycle processes, modernise infrastructure, improve application availability, and strengthen web security through Multi-Factor Authentication. Infosys will use Infosys Cobalt and Infosys Topaz Fabric to support cloud modernisation, unified systems, automation, and data-driven decision-making for 46,000 students and 12,000 staff across global campuses.

key_facts.fact: Infosys and the University of Nottingham announced an extension of their strategic collaboration to ensure high performance and security compliance for the University's critical Student Management System.
Infosys has collaborated with the University of Nottingham since 2017 on the University's Student Information System digital transformation.

The earlier collaboration resulted in reduction of incident volumes by 70 percent, improved system availability and performance by approximately 30 percent, reduced infrastructure costs by over 35 percent, and improved user experience.
Infosys will help the University of Nottingham maintain NottinghamHub, its Student Information System, and enhance key student lifecycle processes.
The collaboration will modernise the University's infrastructure to improve application availability and strengthen web security through Multi-Factor Authentication.
Infosys will leverage Infosys Cobalt and Infosys Topaz Fabric in the collaboration.
The transformation is intended to support a digital-first academic environment for 46,000 students and 12,000 staff across the university's global campuses.

partnerships_or_deals.description: Infosys and the University of Nottingham extended their strategic collaboration for the University's critical Student Management System, including maintenance of NottinghamHub, student lifecycle process enhancements, infrastructure modernisation, application availability improvements, and web security strengthening through Multi-Factor Authentication.

products_or_platforms.name: Infosys Cobalt
products_or_platforms.description: Infosys Cobalt will be used in the University of Nottingham collaboration to support the university's cloud journey and digital infrastructure modernisation.
products_or_platforms.target_market_or_use_case: Cloud modernisation for the University of Nottingham's Student Management System and digital services

products_or_platforms.name: Infosys Topaz Fabric
products_or_platforms.description: Infosys Topaz Fabric will be used in the University of Nottingham collaboration as a multi-layer AI fabric that unifies infrastructure, models, data, applications, and workflows into a composable, agent-ready ecosystem.
products_or_platforms.target_market_or_use_case: Modernising operations, unifying systems, and automating processes for data-driven decision-making
